titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the PAJ records and Bonnie Marranca papers
PAJ Records and Bonnie Marranca Papers
titlestmt.author: by Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday December 2, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: PAJ records and Bonnie Marranca papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection contains correspondence, editorial files, galley proofs, drafts, and photographic prints related to the publication of PAJ Publication titles and PAJ: A Journal of Perfomance and Art (formerly called Performing Arts Journal ). Also included in this collection are correspondence, drafts, ephemera and recorded interviews related to the personal and professional life of Bonnie Marranca, co-founder and editor-in-chief of PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art and editor and publisher of PAJ Publications.

corpname.part: PAJ: a journal of performance and art

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 73-77 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 78 and 87 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Arthur Fournier on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund and the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into seven series: I. PAJ correspondence, 1971-2002. II. PAJ editorial files, 1951-2021. III. PAJ photographic subject files, 1971-2011. IV. Bonnie Marranca personal papers, 1936-2019. V. Bonnie Marranca ephemera, 1965-2020. VI. Audiovisual material, 1973-2009. VII. Computer media, 2001-2009.

bioghist.head: PAJ
bioghist.p: In 1976 Bonnie Marranca and Gautam Dasgupta founded Performing Arts Journal . The journal was retitled to PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art in the late 1990s and is commonly referred to as 'PAJ'. In 1979, Marranca and Dasgupta founded PAJ Publications, an Obie Award-winning publishing house. For over four decades, PAJ published original works of drama, prose, and critical commentary about the performing arts. As Marranca wrote in a January 1998 editorial, "Our aim has always been to dissolve the boundaries of artistic forms and forms of criticism… the great experiments and ideas of writers, painters, dancers, composers, filmmakers, and photographers were enlivened by their collaborations and explorations of different media in relation to each other and to the arts themselves." PAJ Publications held international recognition and influence in scholarship, teaching, and documentation of the performing arts.

PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art was a triannual periodical which captured innovative work in theatre, performance art, dance, video, writing, technology, sound, and music. The journal aimed to collocate dialogue about live arts using a cross-genre and cross-generational approach. Issues included artists' writings, essays, interviews, plays, drawings, original works and coverage of performances, festivals, and books. Marranca and Dasgupta were co-publishers/editors of the press from 1976-2005. Marranca assumed the role of editor-in-chief and publisher from 2006-2024. The periodical was published by MIT Press Journals and was distributed worldwide to over 100 countries.

PAJ Publications began releasing books in 1979 and has published 160 titles in performance, drama, criticism, theatre history and more than 1,000 plays and performance texts. PAJ plays have been translated into over twenty languages.

bioghist.head: Bonnie Marranca
bioghist.p: Bonnie Marranca is the editor-in-chief of PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art , and editor and publisher of PAJ Publications. She holds a B.A. in English from Montclair State College, an M.A in theatre from Hunter College, and partially completed a Ph.D. in theatre at the Graduate Center of The City University of New York (CUNY). She withdrew from the program to start Performing Arts Journal , later renamed PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art , with Gautam Dasgupta in 1976.

Marranca is also an author and scholar who has written five books of theatre criticism and edited fourteen collections of plays, essays, and interviews. Marranca is Professor Emerita of Theatre at The New School/Eugene Lang College for Liberal Arts, a Guggenheim Fellow, and Fulbright Senior Scholar. She taught at many universities domestic and abroad including Columbia University, Princeton University, New York University, Duke University, the University of California-San Diego, Free University (Berlin), Autonomous University/Institute for Theatre (Barcelona), Aarhus University (Denmark), and University of Rio de Janeiro (Brazil).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: PAJ records and Bonnie Marranca papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains two primary sections. The first focuses on the editorial processes related to the production of Performing Arts Journal / PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art issues and PAJ Publications titles. The second section focuses on Bonnie Marranca's personal papers. As a co-founder and editor-in-chief of PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art for over four decades, Marranca's papers are deeply inter-connected with her professional work including correspondence with notable artists, lecture notes, drafts of scholarly articles, recorded interviews, and a collection of print ephemera related to theatre and performing arts shows.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The PAJ records and Bonnie Marranca papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence related to the production and publication of Performing Arts Journal/ PAJ: A Journal of Performance Art and titles released by PAJ Publications. From over three hundred and fifty individuals, some correspondence spans decades and includes personal letters between the correspondent and Bonnie Marranca.

Of note is the folder "Correspondents list," which contains an alphabetized list of a majority of named correspondents in this series. The list is accompanied by short biographical information written by Bonnie Marranca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The folder contains a list of the majority of the named correspondents in the PAJ correspondence series. The list of over three hundred names is accompanied by short biographical information written by Bonnie Marranca. The biographical notes include professions and affiliations to universities, theatres, troupes, and more.

did.unittitle: Weiss, Jeff and Martinez, Carlos Ricardo

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged into five sub-series: PAJ issues, Published titles, Original manuscripts, Current book titles, and Unpublished manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains editorial files for issues of PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art, along with drafts for titles published by PAJ Publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains editorial files for each publication of the journal, PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art up to 2023. The files hold correspondence, drafts, layouts, and other administrative documentation related to the publication of the journal.

arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence, drafts, and galley proofs related to books and journals published by PAJ.

arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains photographs, notes, drafts, galley proofs, and original manuscripts related to books published by PAJ Publications.

Lamb at the Altar: the story of a dance by Deborah Hay

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence, galleys, and manuscripts related to books written by Maria Irene Fornes (1930-2018) and published by PAJ Publications. Fornes was a Cuban-American playwright, theatre director, and teacher who worked in New York City theatre for over four decades.

did.unittitle: A Manual for a Desperate Crossing script
Enter THE NIGHT manuscript for Plays for the End of the Century

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains galley proofs of books published by PAJ Publications prior to time of acquisition in 2023.

Fefu and Her Friends expanded edition

arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains manuscripts that were submitted but not published by PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains over five hundred photographic files used by PAJ: A Journal of Performance and Art and PAJ Publications before the advent and wide use of digital images. The files include documentation of performances by individuals, visual art movements, and theatre companies. The files include photographs used in the magazine Performance Art (later titled LIVE ) which was published by PAJ from 1979-1982.

Companies: Ridiculous Theatrical Company and other Ridiculous work
Dance: New York City Ballet, American Ballet Martha Graham Dance Company

arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, lecture notes, research, drafts, and photographs created by or related to Bonnie Marranca.

arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains programs, flyers, and posters gathered directly from artists and venues including Mabou Mines, Squat Theatre, Wooster Group, Ridiculous Theatrical Co., La Mama, Carolee Schneemann, Philip Glass, and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by first name and/or title of the tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains over eighty audiocassette tapes of original interviews conducted with major figures in the arts including Susan Sontag, Joan Jonas, Herbert Blau, Laurie Anderson, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer media that holds recordings of musical performances, spoken poetry, photographs, and digital proofs.
